title: Need to set security options even if user does not specify options

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mheon @baude We are not applying seccomp.json rules unless the user specifies a security option.  This is needed for 1.9.0.
any way this can have a test so when it fails in v2 we can catch it?
I think we should add this to podman info, then we could at least look for it.
Only reason I caught this, was that I was playing with it to containers.conf running podman in container.

So prior to you change, the StringArray was returning 'nil' instead of an empty array?  If so, that's subtle.  

The remote tstsaren't happy, I restarted the remote one, I don't think it's related.  A new test would also be good.

The problem is the code fails over to the built in.  I guess we could check a syscall that we know is blocked by the default seccomp filters.

mount/unshare perhaps.

But for now lets get this merged, so we can get podman 1.9.0 released.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Prior to this PR, the code was not setting the seccomp.json file to /usr/share/containers/containers.conf unless the user specified ANY --security-opt rule.  We want this set if the user did not override the seccomp file.
@rhatdan got it, thanks.  I wasn't catching that `config.SetSecurityOpts()` was setting stuff to the default location if it got an empty array.
